content.claim: Hungarian SNI population grew continuously from 2012/13 onwards, with documented acceleration from 2021/22: KSH data report 112,007 SNI children in 2022/23 (up from 107,421 prior year) and 104,865 in 2023/24 (up 5,277 from prior year), representing approximately 40% growth over 20 years.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: Absolute totals differ across sources depending on scope/definition (63k primary-school-only vs. 105-112k all levels)
No causal driver of growth is statistically established — multiple competing factors plausible
Methodological inconsistency across reporting streams makes year-on-year micro-comparisons less reliable
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
